persona.the_one_thing: Christian literally teaches Anthropic's public 'Prompting 101' — an iterative, 5-version, evaluate-as-you-go method — so don't pitch Claude adoption abstractly: walk in with ONE concrete orbt agent prompt and its eval, show the before/after, and ask him to critique it. (high confidence; based on his own authored/co-presented material, s9/s7/s10.)

identity.name: Christian Ryan
identity.title: Applied AI Lead, Anthropic (also referenced as Manager, Applied AI Engineering / Product Engineer)

persona.snapshot: Christian Ryan is a product engineer / Applied AI Lead on Anthropic's Applied AI team, based in London, focused on building products and teaching how to get production-quality results out of Claude and agents (s1, s9). His public output centers on one craft: turning prompts and agents from brittle 'tick-box' automation into evaluated, autonomous systems that actually do what they should (s3, s10). He came up through digital/product (KTH education, Voi Technology) rather than AI research, and now operates as Anthropic's hands-on evangelist for real-world Claude deployment (s5, s8).
persona.whats_new: Extremely active in public over the last ~12 months as Anthropic's prompting-and-agents teacher: co-presented 'Prompting 101' with live console demos (May 2026, s9/s27), 'Claude Design by Anthropic Labs' (Apr 2026, s23), a Spotify x Anthropic agentic-development fireside (Mar–Apr 2026, s6/s24/s25), a Weave:London talk on autonomous agents (Mar 2026, s3), and the Anthropic Builder Summit in Bengaluru (Jan 2026, s30). The throughline: he's been on a sustained tour explaining how to build, prompt, and EVALUATE agents at scale.

recent_activity.what: Co-presented Anthropic's 'Prompting 101' — evolving a Claude prompt through 5 versions on a real Swedish insurance-form case
Posted on 'Claude Design by Anthropic Labs'
Authored personal take on AI engineering, 'evangelising digital since day one of my career'
Weave:London talk — how AI agents are becoming autonomous (rigid automation → true autonomy)
Spotify x Anthropic fireside on agentic-first development at scale

top_moves.trait: Prompt iteration is his craft, not a buzzword
top_moves.fact: He co-presents Anthropic's 'Prompting 101', publicly evolving a single prompt through 5 versions on a real customer case (Swedish car-accident insurance forms) (s9).
top_moves.inference: He values demonstrated, staged improvement over claims — and will instantly read whether you actually iterate or just prompt-and-pray.
top_moves.move: Bring ONE real orbt agent prompt and show its evolution (v1 → current) with what each change fixed; let him critique it. This is exactly the language he teaches in.

top_moves.fact: On the AI Sweden podcast he focuses not just on building agents but on 'how to evaluate that they do what they should' (s10); his Weave talk frames the shift from tick-box automation to verified autonomy (s3).
top_moves.inference: For agents that run country strategy and ad creative, his first real question will be 'how do you know the output is right?' — capability alone won't impress him.
top_moves.move: Lead with how orbt evaluates its agents (test cases, guardrails, human-in-loop checks), not just what they can do. Make eval the headline, not a footnote.

top_moves.trait: Thinks in enterprise-scale adoption + org change
top_moves.fact: He sat with Spotify's chief architect to discuss what agentic-first development looks like at scale — tools, infrastructure, and the organizational shifts it demands (s6).
top_moves.inference: He's interested in how teams actually adopt and operationalize agents in production, not lab demos.
top_moves.move: Frame orbt's Slack-delivered agents as a live production-adoption story (real Korean F&B/cosmetics brands using it daily) — a concrete deployment case is what he collects and amplifies.

non_obvious.insight: He's an evangelist/product builder, not an AI researcher — he says he's been 'evangelising digital since day one of my career' (s8) and came from Voi Technology (s5) and a KTH (Stockholm) engineering background (s1). His canonical teaching demo even uses a Swedish insurance case (s9) and he recurs on Swedish-language outlets (s7, s10). The lens to expect: real-world adoption, deployment, and developer experience — not model internals or research novelty.

chemistry.overall_read: Two builders converging on the same live problem — making AI agents that are actually evaluated and production-ready, not brittle demos — so the technical conversation should click fast; moderate confidence, since the overlap is almost entirely professional and the temperaments (public evangelist vs. heads-down proof-over-adjectives builder) cut in opposite directions.

mbti_compat.read: Both NTJ strategists who think in systems and outcomes; the I/E split means Christian drives the room while Sunwoo drives the depth, which tends to interlock rather than compete when there's real substance on the table.
mbti_compat.label: directional

shared_interests.point: Building AI agents as the core of their work, not a side bet
shared_interests.me_evidence: Lists 'AI-native / agentic systems' first among what he cares about and is building orbt's agents that run strategy and creative for brands
shared_interests.them_evidence: 'AI agent development and evaluation' as a core stated skill

shared_interests.point: Insisting agents be evaluated/verified rather than trusted blindly — rigor over vibes
shared_interests.me_evidence: 'I ship, but I want the reasoning to hold up'; frames himself on demonstrated rigor
shared_interests.them_evidence: Focuses on 'how to evaluate that agents do what they should' and teaches a disciplined, staged method

shared_interests.point: Grounding agent work in concrete real-world deployment over abstraction
shared_interests.me_evidence: Ships agents delivered through Slack for real Korean F&B & cosmetics brands expanding cross-border
shared_interests.them_evidence: Anchors teaching in a real Swedish insurance-form workflow and production-quality deployment cases

similar_traits.me_signal: 'lean and fast but rigorous… I want the reasoning to hold up'; Olympiad-style problem solving
similar_traits.them_signal: Teaches a staged 5-version prompt method and insists on evaluating every agent output

complementary_traits.trait: Extraversion / public framing vs. heads-down depth
complementary_traits.me_signal: INTJ builder, 'proof over adjectives,' currently heads-down on the intelligence engine
complementary_traits.them_signal: Sustained public cadence — conference talks, firesides, podcasts, live demos as Anthropic's evangelist
complementary_traits.why_it_helps: Christian's adoption-and-framing instinct can help Sunwoo translate orbt's deep engine into something an audience and customers grasp, while Sunwoo offers exactly the concrete deployed case Christian's teaching feeds on.

complementary_traits.trait: Teaching deployment best-practice vs. owning a deployed product
complementary_traits.me_signal: Runs a real cross-border agent product end-to-end for paying brands
complementary_traits.them_signal: Co-authors Anthropic guidance on writing effective tools for agents and how to prompt/eval at scale
complementary_traits.why_it_helps: Sunwoo can lift production prompt/eval discipline straight from Christian's playbook; Christian gets a live non-Anglophone, cross-border F&B/cosmetics use case that most of his examples don't cover.

potential_friction.me_signal: 'I trust durable partnerships over flashy new ideas'; judges on what's actually built
potential_friction.them_signal: Self-identifies as 'evangelising digital since day one' — lens is adoption and framing
potential_friction.how_to_navigate: Christian should lead with the concrete customer case (his Swedish insurance-form style anchor) rather than the stage pitch; Sunwoo respects demonstrated work, so substance-first defuses the 'is this just rhetoric' reflex.

potential_friction.point: Two strong-willed NTJ operators with firm methods
potential_friction.me_signal: Frames self as the technical engine, Olympiad Medalist, wants the reasoning to hold up
potential_friction.them_signal: ENTJ, directive teacher with a prescribed prompt/eval methodology
potential_friction.how_to_navigate: Keep disagreements on the artifact, not the person — compare eval results and failure cases directly; both default to evidence, so a shared whiteboard problem resolves faster than debate over principles.

interaction_simulation.opening_30s: Sunwoo describes orbt's agents running country-by-country strategy and ad creative for Korean brands through Slack; Christian immediately latches onto 'how do you evaluate that those agents are doing the right thing?' — and they're off.
interaction_simulation.likely_arc: Fast convergence into a peer-level technical exchange: orbt's production eval challenges meet Anthropic's staged prompting/agent-eval frameworks, with mutual credibility building because both anchor in real deployed cases rather than slideware.
interaction_simulation.where_it_could_stall: If Christian stays in broadcast/teaching mode while Sunwoo wants a sparring partner, or if talk drifts toward abstractions and adoption narrative — Sunwoo quietly disengages from anything that reads as 'flashy.' There's also no surfaced non-work common ground to fall back on.
interaction_simulation.how_to_adapt: Sunwoo: bring one specific eval problem from the intelligence engine to make Christian a co-debugger, not a lecturer. Christian: drop the stage voice and go engineer-to-engineer with a real failure case; that's the register Sunwoo rewards.

honest_gaps: chemistry is a low-confidence inference from public text, not a relationship prediction
overlap is almost entirely professional (agents + evaluation); no shared personal interests, geography, or background surfaced in the data
MBTI types are self/illustrative-labeled and used only as directional framing
